description: §60.2/§60.9 — an `<api>` declaration with no `base=` fires E-API-BASE-MISSING (parse-time, W2). `base=` is the external HTTP boundary's address; without it the emitted per-endpoint `fetch(base + path)` callable has no target. A lone `src=` (the A1-reserved ingest attribute, §60.8) does not satisfy the required `base=`.
rationale: §60.2: an `<api>` SHALL declare a `base=` URL. A missing `base=` is E-API-BASE-MISSING (parse-time, W2). The endpoint's `${id}` binds to the declared UserQuery.id and the response is a variant, so only the base code fires.
